textByHash.0c1be3ad3f8ccbc943c9804328c98b21d708c342: Do you though? The subtleties between different species of birds is quite incredible, you see-
textByHash.500a0e51a1dcdf9a6f3c015d6e9fb5d58a10b198: Oh umm, no and yes. I'm forever observing, you see.
textByHash.54906d538fec247bb30935c5d9b6cea02a8a0c6f: Ah yes, well, of course! I've travelled around this island and found the best spots for bird watching. I placed a ring of stones at these locations!
textByHash.5ca33454f1a51b075ee9e08787beaa18ae41a81e: ...Oh, you're still here. What was that? Oh yes, bye for now!
textByHash.61121e254572a38c7ec3212f2eba85eea9bb2a76: Umm, well of course, falcons ARE hunting birds, they mostly catch varying types of kebbits, it's rather mundane.
textByHash.8292cc2e7bb2a2aaa40fbfb5f52a94da390f3740: Traps?! Preposterous! These fine exotic Birds are to be watched and not harmed in any way!
textByHash.85c7c4c04d82971e58deba86e99371f3c399f6ba: Yes, well, perhaps a bit! There are new exotic birds everywhere on this island! I guess I'm getting a little over-excited.
textByHash.a12eb98767bbb0414b0fb757ff5260ba7390a9f4: Oh, erm, birds! Yes, those feathery little flittery things.
textByHash.d0c18dea7561b00c03889d4960700240ee8cbaf2: It's my fathers fault entirely, constantly talking about falconry...
textByHash.d6eba57a6db5bf2084dae723a9e865f1407cff47: You should not interfere with the birds, just watch, no feeding them anything! Not even seeds!
